periods.objective: Classify polynomials by degree and number of terms; write in standard form.
periods.workedExamples: 11-1, Examples 1-3 — Identify Degree and Leading Coefficient; Classify by Degree and Number of Terms; Write in Standard Form
periods.notes: Core classification: degree, leading coefficient, and standard form.

dailyPhases.warmUp: Activate prior knowledge for 11a with one retrieval prompt defining degree and leading coefficient of a polynomial.
dailyPhases.conceptDevelopment: Use 11-1, Examples 1-3 to develop: identify the degree and leading coefficient of a polynomial, classify by degree name and number of terms, and write in standard form with descending powers.
dailyPhases.guidedPractice: Students complete scaffolded problems modeled on 11-1, Examples 1-3: classify polynomials and write in standard form; teacher checks degree identification.
dailyPhases.independentPractice: Students complete aligned practice on polynomial_classification: classification and standard form writing.
dailyPhases.assessment: Exit ticket: one polynomial classification and one standard form writing problem.
dailyPhases.capReflection: CAP reflection: Name the strategy that helped you persist with polynomial classification.

periods.objective: Determine if an expression is a polynomial; apply polynomial models to real-world volume problems.
periods.workedExamples: 11-1, Examples 4-5 — Determine if an Expression Is a Polynomial; Real-World Polynomial Model for Volume
periods.notes: Polynomial test and volume modeling with polynomial expressions.

dailyPhases.warmUp: Activate prior knowledge for 11a with one retrieval prompt checking whether 3x² + 5/x is a polynomial.
dailyPhases.conceptDevelopment: Use 11-1, Examples 4-5 to develop: determine if an expression qualifies as a polynomial by checking for non-negative integer exponents and no division by a variable, and model the volume of a box with polynomial expressions.
dailyPhases.guidedPractice: Students complete scaffolded problems modeled on 11-1, Examples 4-5: test expressions and build volume models; teacher checks the polynomial criteria.
dailyPhases.independentPractice: Students complete aligned practice on polynomial_classification: polynomial test and volume modeling.
dailyPhases.assessment: Exit ticket: one polynomial test and one volume modeling problem.
dailyPhases.capReflection: CAP reflection: Name the strategy that helped you persist with determining polynomial validity.

periods.objective: Add and subtract polynomials by combining like terms.
periods.workedExamples: 11-2, Examples 1-3 — Add Two Polynomials; Subtract Polynomials (Distribute the Negative); Add Three Polynomials
periods.notes: Core addition/subtraction: combining like terms, distributing negatives, and adding three polynomials.

dailyPhases.warmUp: Activate prior knowledge for 11b with one retrieval prompt identifying like terms in 3x² + 5x − 2x² + 7.
dailyPhases.conceptDevelopment: Use 11-2, Examples 1-3 to develop: add two polynomials by combining like terms, subtract polynomials by distributing the negative sign, and add three polynomials simultaneously.
dailyPhases.guidedPractice: Students complete scaffolded problems modeled on 11-2, Examples 1-3: combine like terms for addition and subtraction; teacher checks sign distribution.
dailyPhases.independentPractice: Students complete aligned practice on add_subtract_polynomials: addition, subtraction, and multi-polynomial problems.
dailyPhases.assessment: Exit ticket: one polynomial addition and one polynomial subtraction problem.
dailyPhases.capReflection: CAP reflection: Name the strategy that helped you persist with adding and subtracting polynomials.

periods.objective: Subtract polynomials with nested parentheses; apply polynomial addition to area problems.
periods.workedExamples: 11-2, Examples 4-5 — Subtract with Nested Parentheses; Application: Add Polynomial Areas
periods.notes: Nested-parentheses subtraction and area applications of polynomial addition.

dailyPhases.warmUp: Activate prior knowledge for 11b with one retrieval prompt simplifying (5x³ − 2x² + 4) − (3x³ − x² + 2x − 1).
dailyPhases.conceptDevelopment: Use 11-2, Examples 4-5 to develop: subtract polynomials with nested parentheses requiring two rounds of sign distribution, and add polynomial area expressions to find total planting area.
dailyPhases.guidedPractice: Students complete scaffolded problems modeled on 11-2, Examples 4-5: handle nested subtraction and area setup; teacher checks sign accuracy.
dailyPhases.independentPractice: Students complete aligned practice on add_subtract_polynomials: nested parentheses and area problems.
dailyPhases.assessment: Exit ticket: one nested subtraction problem and one area application.
dailyPhases.capReflection: CAP reflection: Name the strategy that helped you persist with nested parentheses subtraction.

periods.notes: Mastery cycle on 11a-11b.

nonInstructionArtifact.remediationGrouping: Group students by recent objective evidence and misconception patterns.
nonInstructionArtifact.extensionOptions: Offer synthesis problems that combine current objectives with prior units.
nonInstructionArtifact.evidence: Student completes due SRS items and submits one corrected-error reflection.

periods.notes: Mid-unit review on 11a-11b.

nonInstructionArtifact.remediationGrouping: Group students by recent objective evidence and misconception patterns.
nonInstructionArtifact.extensionOptions: Offer synthesis problems that combine current objectives with prior units.
nonInstructionArtifact.evidence: Student completes review problem set and submits one self-identified area for improvement.

periods.objective: Multiply polynomials using the distributive property and FOIL.
periods.workedExamples: 11-3, Examples 1-3 — Multiply Monomial × Polynomial; FOIL: Multiply (x + 3)(x − 5); Multiply Binomial × Trinomial
periods.notes: Core multiplication: monomial distribution, FOIL, and binomial × trinomial.

dailyPhases.warmUp: Activate prior knowledge for 11c with one retrieval prompt multiplying 3x(x² − 4x + 1) using distribution.
dailyPhases.conceptDevelopment: Use 11-3, Examples 1-3 to develop: multiply a monomial by a polynomial using distribution, apply FOIL for binomial × binomial, and extend distribution for binomial × trinomial.
dailyPhases.guidedPractice: Students complete scaffolded problems modeled on 11-3, Examples 1-3: multiply polynomial pairs and combine like terms; teacher checks distribution completeness.
dailyPhases.independentPractice: Students complete aligned practice on multiply_polynomials: monomial, FOIL, and extended multiplication.
dailyPhases.assessment: Exit ticket: one FOIL problem and one binomial × trinomial problem.
dailyPhases.capReflection: CAP reflection: Name the strategy that helped you persist with multiplying polynomials.

periods.objective: Multiply two trinomials; apply polynomial multiplication to area.
periods.workedExamples: 11-3, Examples 4-5 — Multiply Two Trinomials; Application: Multiply for Area
periods.notes: Extended multiplication products and area applications.

dailyPhases.warmUp: Activate prior knowledge for 11c with one retrieval prompt on multiplying every term of the first polynomial by every term of the second.
dailyPhases.conceptDevelopment: Use 11-3, Examples 4-5 to develop: multiply two trinomials systematically by distributing each term and combining like terms, and multiply polynomial expressions to find area.
dailyPhases.guidedPractice: Students complete scaffolded problems modeled on 11-3, Examples 4-5: execute extended products and area setup; teacher checks term tracking.
dailyPhases.independentPractice: Students complete aligned practice on multiply_polynomials: two-trinomial products and area problems.
dailyPhases.assessment: Exit ticket: one two-trinomial multiplication and one area problem.
dailyPhases.capReflection: CAP reflection: Name the strategy that helped you persist with extended polynomial multiplication.

periods.objective: Recognize and apply perfect square trinomial and difference of squares patterns.
periods.workedExamples: 11-4, Examples 1-3 — Expand (x + 5)²; Expand (x − 3)²; Difference of Squares: (x + 4)(x − 4)
periods.notes: Core special product patterns: perfect square trinomials and difference of squares. Split from original dense day to separate foundational patterns from extension activities.

dailyPhases.warmUp: Activate prior knowledge for 11d with one retrieval prompt expanding (x + 2)² and noting the resulting pattern.
dailyPhases.conceptDevelopment: Use 11-4, Examples 1-3 to develop: recognize the perfect square trinomial pattern by expanding (x + 5)² and (x − 3)², and apply the difference of squares pattern to (x + 4)(x − 4).
dailyPhases.guidedPractice: Students complete scaffolded problems modeled on 11-4, Examples 1-3: expand PSTs and factor difference of squares; teacher checks pattern recognition.
dailyPhases.independentPractice: Students complete aligned practice on special_products: PST expansion and difference of squares.
dailyPhases.assessment: Exit ticket: one PST expansion and one difference of squares factoring.
dailyPhases.capReflection: CAP reflection: Name the strategy that helped you persist with special product patterns.

periods.objective: Recognize special product patterns; apply special products for mental math; expand composite perfect square trinomials.
periods.workedExamples: 11-4, Examples 4-6 — Recognize Special Product Patterns; Mental Math; Expand (2x + 3)²
periods.notes: Pattern recognition across mixed types, mental math shortcuts, and composite PST expansion.

dailyPhases.warmUp: Activate prior knowledge for 11d with one retrieval prompt on the difference of squares pattern a² − b² = (a + b)(a − b).
dailyPhases.conceptDevelopment: Use 11-4, Examples 4-6 to develop: identify special product patterns in mixed expressions, use special products for mental math computation, and expand composite perfect square trinomials like (2x + 3)² where the coefficient must be squared.
dailyPhases.guidedPractice: Students complete scaffolded problems modeled on 11-4, Examples 4-6: recognize patterns and expand composites; teacher checks pattern identification.
dailyPhases.independentPractice: Students complete aligned practice on special_products: pattern recognition, mental math, and composite PST.
dailyPhases.assessment: Exit ticket: one pattern recognition problem and one composite PST expansion.
dailyPhases.capReflection: CAP reflection: Name the strategy that helped you persist with pattern recognition and composite PST.

periods.objective: Factor out the GCF and factor by grouping.
periods.workedExamples: 11-5, Examples 1-2 — Factor Out the GCF: 6x³ + 9x²; Factor by Grouping: x³ + 2x² + 3x + 6
periods.notes: GCF extraction and grouping technique introduction.

dailyPhases.warmUp: Activate prior knowledge for 11e with one retrieval prompt on finding the GCF of 6x³ and 9x².
dailyPhases.conceptDevelopment: Use 11-5, Examples 1-2 to develop: factor out the GCF from a polynomial, and factor by grouping four-term polynomials.
dailyPhases.guidedPractice: Students complete scaffolded problems modeled on 11-5, Examples 1-2: extract GCFs and group terms; teacher checks GCF identification.
dailyPhases.independentPractice: Students complete aligned practice on special_products: GCF extraction and factoring by grouping.
dailyPhases.assessment: Exit ticket: one GCF extraction and one factor-by-grouping problem.
dailyPhases.capReflection: CAP reflection: Name the strategy that helped you persist with GCF and grouping.

periods.objective: Factor trinomials with leading coefficient 1 and ≠ 1.
periods.workedExamples: 11-5, Examples 3-4 — Factor x² + 7x + 12 (Leading Coefficient 1); Factor 2x² + 7x + 3 (Leading Coefficient ≠ 1)
periods.notes: Trinomial factoring: multiply-to-c method and AC method with grouping.

dailyPhases.warmUp: Activate prior knowledge for 11f with one retrieval prompt on finding two numbers that multiply to 12 and add to 7.
dailyPhases.conceptDevelopment: Use 11-5, Examples 3-4 to develop: factor x² + bx + c by finding two numbers that multiply to c and add to b, and factor ax² + bx + c with leading coefficient ≠ 1 using the AC method.
dailyPhases.guidedPractice: Students complete scaffolded problems modeled on 11-5, Examples 3-4: set up and execute trinomial factoring for both cases; teacher checks factor-pair identification.
dailyPhases.independentPractice: Students complete aligned practice on factoring_trinomials: leading-coefficient-1 and leading-coefficient-not-1 problems.
dailyPhases.assessment: Exit ticket: one trinomial factoring problem with leading coefficient 1 and one with ≠ 1.
dailyPhases.capReflection: CAP reflection: Name the strategy that helped you persist with factoring trinomials.

periods.objective: Factor difference of squares and factor completely.
periods.workedExamples: 11-5, Examples 5-6 — Factor x² − 25 (Difference of Squares); Factor Completely: 2x³ − 8x
periods.notes: Difference-of-squares factoring and complete factoring with GCF + pattern. Split from original dense day to separate factoring techniques from zero product property solving.

dailyPhases.warmUp: Activate prior knowledge for 11g with one retrieval prompt factoring x² − 16 as a difference of squares.
dailyPhases.conceptDevelopment: Use 11-5, Examples 5-6 to develop: factor x² − 25 as a difference of squares, and factor completely by extracting GCF first then applying a pattern.
dailyPhases.guidedPractice: Students complete scaffolded problems modeled on 11-5, Examples 5-6: factor difference of squares and factor completely; teacher checks factoring sequence.
dailyPhases.independentPractice: Students complete aligned practice on factoring_trinomials: difference of squares and complete factoring.
dailyPhases.assessment: Exit ticket: one difference-of-squares problem and one factor-completely problem.
dailyPhases.capReflection: CAP reflection: Name the strategy that helped you persist with difference of squares and factoring completely.

periods.objective: Solve polynomial equations using the zero product property.
periods.workedExamples: 11-6, Examples 1-3 — Solve x² − 5x = 0; Solve x² + 3x − 10 = 0; Solve 2x² − 8 = 0 (GCF First)
periods.notes: Zero product property solving: factoring then setting each factor to zero.

dailyPhases.warmUp: Activate prior knowledge for 11g with one retrieval prompt on the zero product property: if AB = 0 then A = 0 or B = 0.
dailyPhases.conceptDevelopment: Use 11-6, Examples 1-3 to develop: solve polynomial equations by factoring and applying the zero product property, always checking for a GCF before other factoring methods.
dailyPhases.guidedPractice: Students complete scaffolded problems modeled on 11-6, Examples 1-3: factor and solve using zero product property; teacher checks factoring sequence.
dailyPhases.independentPractice: Students complete aligned practice on zero_product_property: solving polynomial equations by factoring.
dailyPhases.capReflection: CAP reflection: Name the strategy that helped you persist with the zero product property.

periods.notes: Higher-degree polynomial solving by extracting GCF and applying difference of squares.

dailyPhases.warmUp: Activate prior knowledge for 11h with one retrieval prompt on factoring x³ − x by first extracting the GCF x.
dailyPhases.conceptDevelopment: Use 11-6, Example 4 to develop: solve a cubic equation by factoring out the GCF and then applying difference of squares to find all three roots.
dailyPhases.guidedPractice: Students complete scaffolded problems modeled on 11-6, Example 4: factor cubic expressions and apply zero product property; teacher checks factoring steps.
dailyPhases.independentPractice: Students complete aligned practice on zero_product_property: cubic equation solving.
dailyPhases.assessment: Exit ticket: one cubic equation to solve.
dailyPhases.capReflection: CAP reflection: Name the strategy that helped you persist with solving cubic polynomial equations.

periods.objective: Find zeros of real-world polynomial models.
periods.workedExamples: 11-6, Example 5 — Real-World: Find Zeros of a Polynomial Model
periods.notes: Applying factoring and zero product property to find and interpret zeros of real-world polynomial models.

dailyPhases.warmUp: Activate prior knowledge for 11h with one retrieval prompt on interpreting what a zero of a function means in context.
dailyPhases.conceptDevelopment: Use 11-6, Example 5 to develop: find the zeros of a real-world polynomial model by factoring and interpreting the solutions in context (e.g., when a ball hits the ground).
dailyPhases.guidedPractice: Students complete scaffolded problems modeled on 11-6, Example 5: set up, factor, solve, and interpret zeros; teacher checks contextual interpretation.
dailyPhases.independentPractice: Students complete aligned practice on zero_product_property: real-world zero-finding and interpretation.
dailyPhases.assessment: Exit ticket: one real-world polynomial zero problem with interpretation.
dailyPhases.capReflection: CAP reflection: Name the strategy that helped you persist with finding zeros of real-world models.

periods.notes: Mathematical practices focus: factoring strategy selection, error analysis in sign distribution, and method justification.

nonInstructionArtifact.groupTask: Mathematical practices focus: factoring strategy selection, error analysis in sign distribution, and method justification.
nonInstructionArtifact.sourceWorkedExamples: Use the prior instruction periods named in the notes as the problem bank.
nonInstructionArtifact.deliverable: Group solution poster or shared explanation with one strategy comparison.
nonInstructionArtifact.facilitationNotes: Assign roles for strategy selection, factoring execution, sign checking, and explanation.
nonInstructionArtifact.completionCriteria: Correct method, accurate result, clear justification, and response to peer question.

periods.notes: Mastery cycle on 11c-11h.

nonInstructionArtifact.remediationGrouping: Group students by recent objective evidence and misconception patterns.
nonInstructionArtifact.extensionOptions: Offer synthesis problems that combine current objectives with prior units.
nonInstructionArtifact.evidence: Student completes due SRS items and submits one corrected-error reflection.

periods.notes: Unit 11 review across 11a-11h.

nonInstructionArtifact.remediationGrouping: Group students by recent objective evidence and misconception patterns.
nonInstructionArtifact.extensionOptions: Offer synthesis problems that combine current objectives with prior units.
nonInstructionArtifact.evidence: Student completes review problem set and submits one self-identified area for improvement.

periods.notes: Unit 11 assessment.
Unit 11 assessment — retake or extended time.
